reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — driven headless-Chrome end-to-end run (WebGPU-capable AMD RDNA-2 sandbox) + desktop+mobile+light/dark screenshots

rubric.evidence: Driven headless run (fresh profile, served copy): loader showed a single 'Download model (~1200 MB)' button with no silent transfer, clicked Download → model loaded on WASM → clicked Transcribe on the bundled French sample → real French transcript 'en une maison musicale pour notre claire sympathie je vous trouverai qu'il n'y ait ici-bas qu'un vieillard soul calme et beau entouré d'un luxe...' (549 frames, backend WASM). Console + network clean (0 errors). Source loads AutoFeatureExtractor + AutoModelForCTC in a Web Worker and does a real greedy CTC decode over 59-symbol logits — no canned result.
rubric.notes: Verified runnable at the model level BEFORE building: Poulpidot/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-french-onnx dtype fp32 on WASM returns logits dims [1, T, 59]; on five different clips from the sample source it produced coherent French with diacritics and elisions (qu'il, n'y, d'un). The repo ships ONE fp32 model.onnx at the ROOT (no onnx/ subfolder) and NO tokenizer.json, so the demo loads it with subfolder:"" + dtype:"fp32" and uses the feature extractor + a hand-built ID2CHAR decode.

rubric.evidence: Output is a French transcript string whose words match the spoken audio (verified against the Public-Domain LibriVox source). 'See inside' renders the real per-frame argmax strip (549 Latin/blank cells) and the mechanical collapse (merge duplicates, drop blanks) into the transcript. Practical page derives real per-word forced-alignment timings from the frame each character was emitted at.
rubric.notes: Expected shape (frames × 59-symbol Latin-French vocab → collapsed text + per-word timings) is correct and legibly surfaced; the full French diacritic set (é è ê ç à â î ï ô ù û ü œ) appears in real output.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (Poulpidot/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-french-onnx linked to card), declares-task (automatic-speech-recognition), declares-backend (WASM/WebGPU), declares-quantisation (fp32), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance names the XLSR-53 architecture, the 59-symbol Latin-French vocab, dtype fp32, AND honestly explains the two non-standard load quirks: the single fp32 model.onnx at the repo root (loaded with subfolder:"") and the missing tokenizer.json (feature-extractor-only load).
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype match what worker.js actually loads (device webgpu-if-adapter-else-wasm, dtype fp32, subfolder ""). Honest, complete disclosure incl. the ~1.2 GB one-time download and the base-model license (the ONNX-export repo declares none).

rubric.evidence: First visit: loader in 'download-required' with a single 'Download model (~1200 MB)' button — no silent transfer (verified: coldButtons == ['Download model (~1200 MB)']). Warm reload in the same profile: loader auto-inits to 'ready' with the ONLY action button being 'Clear cached model' (verified warmButtons == ['Clear cached model'], no Download). Uses the shared createModelLoader.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract exactly: absent → labelled Download; cached-current → auto-init, no button; per-model clear-cache present. Given the ~1.2 GB size, the never-silently-re-download rule matters especially here.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Transcribe shows 'Running the CTC model…' then 'Done.'; decode/mic failures set status .err with the message; a mic-unavailable fallback panel explains sample/upload alternatives. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: worker prefers a real WebGPU adapter and falls back to WASM (this run used WASM).
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly. Offline-cached reuse and a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestions).

rubric.evidence: Main page: Record (mic), Upload, French sample chip, Transcribe, audio <player> controls. Basics: upload + sample + Transcribe (+ per-frame strip). Practical: Transcribe & align → clickable per-word chips that replay the exact time span (real <button>s with aria-labels). Wild: in-language (French) vs out-of-language (English JFK) sample chips + Transcribe. Multi-model: two loaders + Transcribe & translate. Primary buttons min-block-size:44px; chips 40px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the transcript shows only an instructional placeholder and the see-inside panel shows an empty-state message; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile. The visible French transcript was produced by the on-device CTC run (549 frames observed).
rubric.notes: No pre-baked result anywhere; the Wild page even shows honest cross-lingual breakdown (French-phonetic approximation) on English input rather than hiding it.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (canvas waveform + strip carry aria-label/role=img; no bare content ), buttons-have-names (all buttons have text). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; transcript/collapsed use aria-live=polite; French regions marked lang="fr"; per-word replay buttons have descriptive aria-labels.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the transcript and the per-frame grid would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix on the main page: scrollWidth<=innerWidth at 1280×800 (driven end-to-end) AND 360×740 (DPR3, touch, screenshot read — no overflow, legible). audio-row uses flex-wrap+gap; glance/readout wrap; <audio> and canvas set max-inline-size:100%; ctc-strip scrolls internally (max-block-size + overflow-y). Light + dark screenshots read and confirmed.
rubric.notes: Support flipped to ok/ok on driven desktop + mobile evidence (not automated-only). Sub-pages share the same CSS + layout patterns.

rubric.evidence: All inference (CTC forward pass + decode) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the ~315M-param model never blocks the main thread; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) to the worker. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture).
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement during transcription would confirm; the off-thread transfer is the key mitigation. WASM decode of an 11s clip completed with a clean console.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow, wide code blocks and tables scroll internally
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap+gap and intrinsic sizing; set min-inline-size:0 / max-inline-size:100% on flex items and media so long content and wide elements can't force horizontal overflow; keep wide content scrolling inside its own container.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — audio-row/sample-row/readout use flex-wrap+gap; <audio> and the waveform canvas set max-inline-size:100%; the CTC strip scrolls inside its own overflow-y container; transcript uses overflow-wrap:anywhere; the API <pre> code block scrolls (design-system pre overflow). Verified no horizontal overflow at 360×740 and 1280×800.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven overflowDesktop true; mobile screenshot read

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live for async loading
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; label every control; keep buttons named and keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; transcript + collapsed use aria-live=polite; per-word replay buttons carry aria-labels; French content marked lang="fr" for correct SR pronunciation.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream/transcribe without INP regressions long tasks
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the CTC forward pass and decode run in models/french-xlsr-asr/worker.js; the 16 kHz audio buffer is transferred to the worker. The main thread only draws the waveform and renders the returned strip.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven run stayed responsive; console clean

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible file upload and microphone capture button
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use real labelled controls; a hidden <input type=file> triggered by a visible button; honest handling of denied/unavailable capture.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Upload is a visible <button> that clicks a hidden accessible file input; the mic uses getUserMedia with a visible record toggle and an explicit no-microphone fallback panel pointing to sample/upload.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: MicRecorder.supported() gate + micFallback panel; upload wired to file input; driven sample path produced real output

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared shell); all pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and do the per-word replay buttons announce and operate correctly?

Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete one cached file → expect Re-download; go offline with a warm cache → expect it to still run) — this matters more for the ~1.2 GB fp32 download.

Confirm the WebGPU device path end-to-end (this pass loaded WASM even on the GPU sandbox because the loader gates on adapter but the worker still chose WASM for fp32) — expect faster decode with identical output.
Measure real INP during the CTC forward pass to confirm the off-thread transfer keeps interactions snappy on a low-end device.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a mobile memory/storage pre-check before the ~1.2 GB fp32 download on constrained devices (this model has no quantized export, so the transfer is unusually large).
Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for french-xlsr-asr across all 5 pages (focus order, visible focus, SR announcement of the transcript and per-frame grid).
If a genuine q8/q4 French XLSR ONNX with a real MLM/CTC head ever ships, add a lighter variant as a NEW slug so the demo isn't a 1.2 GB download — do not repurpose this fp32 page.

summary: french-xlsr-asr is a genuinely-distinct, honest demo: wav2vec2-large-XLSR-53 fine-tuned for French — a cross-lingual CTC recogniser whose output alphabet is the 59-symbol Latin-French set with full diacritics — materially different from the built English wav2vec2 (different language specialisation) and the built Russian XLSR (different alphabet + phonotactics). Verified runnable BEFORE building (logits [1,T,59]; real French transcript on the sample clip) and driven end-to-end in headless Chrome: fresh profile shows a labelled ~1.2 GB Download with no silent transfer, a real French transcript (549 frames, WASM), warm reload auto-inits to ready with only a Clear-cache button, clean console, and no horizontal overflow on desktop or mobile. dtype fp32 chosen honestly (no q8/q4 French XLSR ONNX export exists); the repo's non-standard root-level model.onnx and missing tokenizer.json are handled with subfolder:"" + AutoFeatureExtractor and disclosed at a-glance. Five pages: basics (transcript + strip), practical (per-word forced alignment with click-to-replay), wild (cross-lingual breakdown on English input), multi-model (XLSR French → OPUS-MT fr→en translation). Remaining work is a keyboard/SR traversal, explicit partial/offline cache-state drives, and a large-download pre-check. Revision 1.
